visa_types.name: Tourist
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please
                                                                        click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ecu/en/ita/travel-insurance).
- Visa Application Form
- Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased,
Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ecu/en/ita/travel-insurance)
- Recent carnet style photo. Please “click here ” for photo specification
- Valid passport (remember it must remain valid for at least 6 months from the visa application date and 3 months from date of end of your trip;
- Health insurance certificate covering at least an amount of 30.000 Euros for urgent hospital and repatriation expenses
- List of other documents (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1M4BWoIb0EYfLm7ezwZcfW/39cde0395186e0df08b15d9b349893cf/requisitos-new-eng.pdf)
- Invitation Letter
Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7EOLER7WiPdqtoXaKGONQ6/254a37433de0818757b4af6a15488ada/dichiarazione-di-ospitalita-amici-familiari-nuovo.pdf)
visa_types.visa_fees:
This section contains information about visa application fees and service charges, if you are applying for a visa at the visa service Center to come to Italy from Ecuador.
Dependents are charged the same fee as the main applicant unless otherwise stated. All dependents that are travelling must pay the application fee and service charges whether or not they
                                                                        are included in the main applicant's passport with some exception.
You can use the quick links listed here to take you the information or you can scroll down the page.
visa_types.processing_time:
- Processing times in Ecuador
The Majority of standard visa applications are processed within 3-5 working days in Quito, and 8 working days in provinces, from the date your application is submitted to the
                                                                                Consular Department of the Embassy of Italy in Quito.

Important notes: 
- Visa applications are assessed individually and processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application will depend on several factors and because of
                                                                                    this you should not to make your travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Italy until you have received your Schengen visa.
- Your passport can be retained by the Consular Department of the Italy Embassy in Quito for 30 days for processing of visa application
- You may require to appear for personal interview, should consulate in Quito require
- When to prepare your application
The Consular Department of the Embassy of Italy in Ecuador advises you to allow enough time to prepare your visa application. Some applicants can take 1  to 2                                                                                weeks to prepare their visa application and you should allow enough time to read the guidance on the visa category you are applying for, complete your visa application form,
                                                                                prepare your supporting documents and arrange for translations (if necessary). You can submit your visa application up to 3 months in advance of your planned travel date.
                                                                                
Queries about processing times
You can track the status of your application by using the Track your Application facility on this website and by using the SMS text alert facility (fee payable).you can contact
                                                                                Visa Service Center by using the details available on the Contact Us page of this website. 
Role of Visa Application Center in processing

Please note Visa Application Center and none of its staff has any role in the decision making process. Processing of visa application is completed by the staffs at the Embassy
                                                                                of Italy in Quito and Guayaquil.
visa_types.overview: When applying for a visa, you are required to bring 01 photograph as part of completing and submitting your visa application.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The short term visas (Schengen) enabled for transit or stay in Italy, Germany, Austria, Belgium, Denmark, Slovakia, Slovenia, Estonia, Finland, France, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Spain,
                                                                        Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Norway, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Czech Republic, Sweden and Switzerland, which must not exceed three months (90 days) in a period of six
                                                                        months (180 days) from the first date of entry into the territory of the Schengen States.
All minors need to be represented by both parents to apply for a visa, only exception is if one of them have full legal custody (patria postestad)
They are subject to the obligation to obtain this visa for nationals of countries that are included in the list of countries subject to visa requirements for the crossing of external
                                                                        borders.
For more information please follow the this link: http://www.esteri.it/visti/index_eng.asp (http://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/home/en)

## Visa Fees
This section contains information about visa application fees and service charges, if you are applying for a visa at the visa service Center to come to Italy from Ecuador.
Dependents are charged the same fee as the main applicant unless otherwise stated. All dependents that are travelling must pay the application fee and service charges whether or not they
                                                                        are included in the main applicant's passport with some exception.
You can use the quick links listed here to take you the information or you can scroll down the page.

## Visa Fee
The below rates apply at the visa service Center . They may be varied at any time. Charges are non-refundable and may only be paid in cash US Dollars only. Credit cards and other forms
                                                                        of payment are not accepted. Whenever possible the cashier should be presented with the exact money.
You must deposit Visa fee and Service charges to Bank of Pichincha within 48 hours from the date of your appointment with the visa application Center. Please ensure you must carry two
                                                                        deposit receipts issued by the bank. One per Italian Embassy (Visa fee) and other for Vieffsglobal (Services Charges)

[Table: Tourist]

## Service Charges

[Table: Tourist]
Please Note:
- Service charges are inclusive of VAT levied per application over and above the visa fees.

## Optional value added services
To add value to our customer, we offer some value added service. You will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the visa application fee when you attend the visa service Center only.
                                                                        To know about these services please refer the additional service page of this website. 

## Visa Fees waived categories
- Children under six years
- Students from third countries traveling for the purpose of scientific research as defined in Recommendation 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and the Council of 28 September
                                                                            2005 in order to facilitate the issue by Member States of uniform for short to researchers from third countries traveling within the Community for purposes of scientific research;
- Representatives of non-profit organizations that are not older than twenty years and will participate in seminars, conferences or sporting events, cultural or educational purposes,
                                                                            organized by non-profit organizations.
- Family members of citizens of the European Union, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway and Switzerland, provided traveling with the past or to be reunited with them. For family members
                                                                            of citizens of the European Union, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway and Switzerland are considered: the spouse or partner to maintain a union similar to marriage registered in a
                                                                            public register established for this purpose in one of the above countries, their descendants and those of your spouse under 21 years or older of that age living from his office
                                                                            or incapable; and direct ascendants and those of his spouse or registered partner to live to their charge.
Note: Please note that you may be charged visa fee even though you meet the above information. This will be done only in consultation with the Consular Department of
                                                                        the Embassy of Italy in Quito, Ecuador 

## Refund process
Visa application fee reflects the administrative costs of processing an application. Your visa application fee will not be refunded unless:
- You withdraw your application in writing before any processing takes place; or
- Embassy authorizes a refund
Please note that visa fee and service charges are not refunded if your application is refused by the Embassy of Italy, or if you withdraw you application while it is under processing
                                                                        at the Embassy of Italy in Quito, Ecuador. 

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please
                                                                        click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ecu/en/ita/travel-insurance).
- Visa Application Form
- Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased,
Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ecu/en/ita/travel-insurance)
- Recent carnet style photo. Please “click here ” for photo specification
- Valid passport (remember it must remain valid for at least 6 months from the visa application date and 3 months from date of end of your trip;
- Health insurance certificate covering at least an amount of 30.000 Euros for urgent hospital and repatriation expenses
- List of other documents (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1M4BWoIb0EYfLm7ezwZcfW/39cde0395186e0df08b15d9b349893cf/requisitos-new-eng.pdf)
- Invitation Letter
Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7EOLER7WiPdqtoXaKGONQ6/254a37433de0818757b4af6a15488ada/dichiarazione-di-ospitalita-amici-familiari-nuovo.pdf)

## Photo Specifications

## Overview
When applying for a visa, you are required to bring 01 photograph as part of completing and submitting your visa application.

## Photo Requirements
You need to submit 01 one) photographs. One photo must be passed ( not stapled) on the visa application form and another photo needs to be given to Visa application centre at the time
                                                                        of submission. Your visa application photo must meet certain criteria regarding size and content. Submitting photos that do not meet these criteria can delay the visa application
                                                                        process. Your photographs must meet the guidelines shown in the image below:
Head Size
Your head height, measured from the top of your head, including the hair, to the bottom of your chin, must be between 50% and 70% of the photo's total height. Your eye height, measured
                                                                        from the bottom of the photo to the level of your eyes, should be between 55% and 70% - or roughly 2/3 - of the photo's height.
Photo Dimensions
Your photo must be 4.5 x 3.5 cm and square-shaped, meaning the photo's height must be the same as its width.

## 7 Steps to Successful Photo
Head Orientation
Head orientation is important when taking your visa photo. Frame yourself within the photo so that your full face shows. Face directly toward the camera and make sure your eyes are open.
Fill the Frame
Your photo must show your entire head, from the top of your hair to the bottom of your chin.
Stay Centered
Center your head within the frame.
Show Your Eyes
Make sure your eyes are open.
Background &  Quality
The area behind you in the photo should be uncluttered and either white . Sit in front of a plain white background for best results. Photo quality must be of high resolution and must
                                                                        be printed on normal photographic paper ( photo printing)
Eliminate Shadows
Sit so that your face is fully-lit and there are no distracting shadows across your face or in the background
Relax and Look  Natural
Be sure to have a natural expression on your face when you take your photo, like those shown here:

## Photograph facility at VAC
You also have an option to get your photograph clicked at the VAC. To know about photograph charges, please visit additional services webpage of this site.

## More Information
- For more information about visa application photo specifications, visit click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4aXl2m33HReRwRycqYHsXU/2c18283a88643f38892a5615bc64d1ef/photo_spec.pdf)
- Valid passport (remember it must remain valid for at least 6 months from date of end of your trip;
- Health insurance certificate covering at least an amount of 30.000 Euros for urgent hospital and repatriation expenses
- You have now an option to purchase Travel Medical Insurance at competitive prices either while you are the visa application centre to submit your application or online.
- At the VAC: Contact Travel Ace Assistance desk to know more and purchase of travel medical insurance as per your travel plan.
- Online (http://www.travel-ace.com/ecuador/formularios/vfs/index.php) : Purchase Travel Medical insurance before arriving
                                                                            to visa application Centre.
- Other important information:
- This service will be offered by Travel Ace Assistance at the Visa application centre
- This service is purely optional in nature
- You will have to fill the consent form before purchasing the Insurance at the Visa application Centre
- Product information commercial (http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7IH2SQn9Vdw&list=UUV4-rVBLg9FrRLHjJcBWOpg&index=1&feature=plcp)
- The Travel and Health Insurance is operated by Travel Ace Assistance company and they have the sole responsibility of any claims or issues with the Insurance Policies
- Service will be operational with effect from September 10, 2012

## Processing Time
- Processing times in Ecuador
The Majority of standard visa applications are processed within 3-5 working days in Quito, and 8 working days in provinces, from the date your application is submitted to the
                                                                                Consular Department of the Embassy of Italy in Quito.

Important notes: 
- Visa applications are assessed individually and processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application will depend on several factors and because of
                                                                                    this you should not to make your travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Italy until you have received your Schengen visa.
- Your passport can be retained by the Consular Department of the Italy Embassy in Quito for 30 days for processing of visa application
- You may require to appear for personal interview, should consulate in Quito require
- When to prepare your application
The Consular Department of the Embassy of Italy in Ecuador advises you to allow enough time to prepare your visa application. Some applicants can take 1  to 2                                                                                weeks to prepare their visa application and you should allow enough time to read the guidance on the visa category you are applying for, complete your visa application form,
                                                                                prepare your supporting documents and arrange for translations (if necessary). You can submit your visa application up to 3 months in advance of your planned travel date.
                                                                                
Queries about processing times
You can track the status of your application by using the Track your Application facility on this website and by using the SMS text alert facility (fee payable).you can contact
                                                                                Visa Service Center by using the details available on the Contact Us page of this website. 
Role of Visa Application Center in processing

Please note Visa Application Center and none of its staff has any role in the decision making process. Processing of visa application is completed by the staffs at the Embassy
                                                                                of Italy in Quito and Guayaquil.

## Visa Application Form
Spanish Version (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/10o5Akws4wJnRXYoBCOttp/c2d2be498e04c14bf2e8dbbd8f02864b/formulario-schengen-italia-nuevo.pdf) 
If you want to download the visa application form:
- Click on the link of the form
- When the page appears, save the form on your computer
OR
- Press the right button of your mouse/mouse when the cursor on the appropriate link
- Select< save>
- Save the form on your computer.

- Processing times in Ecuador
The Majority of standard visa applications are processed within 3-5 working days in Quito, and 8 working days in provinces, from the date your application is submitted to the
                                                                                Consular Department of the Embassy of Italy in Quito.
Important notes: 
- Visa applications are assessed individually and processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application will depend on several factors and because of
                                                                                    this you should not to make your travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Italy until you have received your Schengen visa.
- Your passport can be retained by the Consular Department of the Italy Embassy in Quito for 30 days for processing of visa application
- You may require to appear for personal interview, should consulate in Quito require
When to prepare your application

The Consular Department of the Embassy of Italy in Ecuador advises you to allow enough time to prepare your visa application. Some applicants can take 1  to 2                                                                                weeks to prepare their visa application and you should allow enough time to read the guidance on the visa category you are applying for, complete your visa application form,
                                                                                prepare your supporting documents and arrange for translations (if necessary). You can submit your visa application up to 3 months in advance of your planned travel date.
                                                                                
Queries about processing times

You can track the status of your application by using the Track your Application facility on this website and by using the SMS text alert facility (fee payable).you can contact
                                                                                Visa Service Center by using the details available on the Contact Us page of this website. 
Role of Visa Application Center in processing

Please note Visa Application Center and none of its staff has any role in the decision making process. Processing of visa application is completed by the staffs at the Embassy
                                                                                of Italy in Quito and Guayaquil.
visa_types.overview: When applying for a visa, you are required to bring 01 photograph as part of completing and submitting your visa application.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The short term visas (Schengen) enabled for transit or stay in Italy, Germany, Austria, Belgium, Denmark, Slovakia, Slovenia, Estonia, Finland, France, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Spain,
                                                                        Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Norway, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Czech Republic, Sweden and Switzerland, which must not exceed three months (90 days) in a period of six
                                                                        months (180 days) from the first date of entry into the territory of the Schengen States.
All minors need to be represented by both parents to apply for a visa, only exception is if one of them has full legal custody (patria postestad)
They are subject to the obligation to obtain this visa for nationals of countries that are included in the list of countries subject to visa requirements for the crossing of external
                                                                        borders.
For more information please follow the this link: http://www.esteri.it/visti/index_eng.asp (http://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/home/en)

## Visa Fees
This section contains information about visa application fees and service charges, if you are applying for a visa at the visa service Center to come to Italy from Ecuador.
Dependents are charged the same fee as the main applicant unless otherwise stated. All dependents that are travelling must pay the application fee and service charges whether or not they
                                                                        are included in the main applicant's passport with some exception.
You can use the quick links listed here to take you the information or you can scroll down the page.

## Visa Fee
The below rates apply at the visa service Center . They may be varied at any time. Charges are non-refundable and may only be paid in cash US Dollars only. Credit cards and other forms
                                                                        of payment are not accepted. Whenever possible the cashier should be presented with the exact money.
You must deposit Visa fee and Service charges to Bank of Pichincha within 48 hours from the date of your appointment with the visa application Center. Please ensure you must carry two
                                                                        deposit receipts issued by the bank. One per Italian Embassy (Visa fee) and other for Vieffsglobal (Services Charges)

[Table: Sports and other Events ]

## Service Charges

[Table: Sports and other Events ]
Please Note:
- Service charges are inclusive of VAT levied per application over and above the visa fees.

## Optional value added services
To add value to our customer, we offer some value added service. You will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the visa application fee when you attend the visa service Center only.
                                                                        To know about these services please refer the additional service page of this website. 

## Visa Fees waived categories
- Children under six years
- Students from third countries traveling for the purpose of scientific research as defined in Recommendation 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and the Council of 28 September
                                                                            2005 in order to facilitate the issue by Member States of uniform for short to researchers from third countries traveling within the Community for purposes of scientific research;
- Representatives of non-profit organizations that are not older than twenty years and will participate in seminars, conferences or sporting events, cultural or educational purposes,
                                                                            organized by non-profit organizations.
- Family members of citizens of the European Union, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway and Switzerland, provided traveling with the past or to be reunited with them. For family members
                                                                            of citizens of the European Union, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway and Switzerland are considered: the spouse or partner to maintain a union similar to marriage registered in a
                                                                            public register established for this purpose in one of the above countries, their descendants and those of your spouse under 21 years or older of that age living from his office
                                                                            or incapable; and direct ascendants and those of his spouse or registered partner to live to their charge.
Note: Please note that you may be charged visa fee even though you meet the above information. This will be done only in consultation with the Consular Department of
                                                                        the Embassy of Italy in Quito, Ecuador 

## Refund process
Visa application fee reflects the administrative costs of processing an application. Your visa application fee will not be refunded unless:
- You withdraw your application in writing before any processing takes place; or
- Embassy authorizes a refund
Please note that visa fee and service charges are not refunded if your application is refused by the Embassy of Italy, or if you withdraw you application while it is under processing
                                                                        at the Embassy of Italy in Quito, Ecuador. 

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please
                                                                        click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ecu/en/ita/travel-insurance).
- Visa Application Form
- Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased,
Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ecu/en/ita/travel-insurance)
- Recent carnet style photo. Please “click here” for photo specification
- Valid passport (remember it must remain valid for at least 6 months from the visa application date and 3 months from date of end of your trip;
- Health insurance certificate covering at least an amount of 30.000 Euros for urgent hospital and repatriation expenses
- List of other documents (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5lDHlrDCXu7AvRj0IygR1Z/0e208173c11a8c29bcdaddfc2102e76f/sports_visa_form.pdf)

## Photo Specifications

## Overview
When applying for a visa, you are required to bring 01 photograph as part of completing and submitting your visa application.

## Photo Requirements
You need to submit 01 one) photographs. One photo must be passed ( not stapled) on the visa application form and another photo needs to be given to Visa application centre at the time
                                                                        of submission. Your visa application photo must meet certain criteria regarding size and content. Submitting photos that do not meet these criteria can delay the visa application
                                                                        process. Your photographs must meet the guidelines shown in the image below:
Head Size
Your head height, measured from the top of your head, including the hair, to the bottom of your chin, must be between 50% and 70% of the photo's total height. Your eye height, measured
                                                                        from the bottom of the photo to the level of your eyes, should be between 55% and 70% - or roughly 2/3 - of the photo's height.
Photo Dimensions
Your photo must be 4.5 x 3.5 cm and square-shaped, meaning the photo's height must be the same as its width.

## 7 Steps to Successful Photo
Head Orientation
Head orientation is important when taking your visa photo. Frame yourself within the photo so that your full face shows. Face directly toward the camera and make sure your eyes are open.
Fill the Frame
Your photo must show your entire head, from the top of your hair to the bottom of your chin.
Stay Centered
Center your head within the frame.
Show Your Eyes
Make sure your eyes are open.
Background &  Quality
The area behind you in the photo should be uncluttered and either white . Sit in front of a plain white background for best results. Photo quality must be of high resolution and must
                                                                        be printed on normal photographic paper ( photo printing)
Eliminate Shadows
Sit so that your face is fully-lit and there are no distracting shadows across your face or in the background
Relax and Look  Natural
Be sure to have a natural expression on your face when you take your photo, like those shown here:

## Photograph facility at VAC
You also have an option to get your photograph clicked at the VAC. To know about photograph charges, please visit additional services webpage of this site.

## More Information
- For more information about visa application photo specifications, visit click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4aXl2m33HReRwRycqYHsXU/2c18283a88643f38892a5615bc64d1ef/photo_spec.pdf)
- Valid passport (remember it must remain valid for at least 6 months from date of end of your trip;
- Health insurance certificate covering at least an amount of 30.000 Euros for urgent hospital and repatriation expenses
- You have now an option to purchase Travel Medical Insurance at competitive prices either while you are the visa application centre to submit your application or online.
- At the VAC
: Contact Travel Ace Assistance desk to know more and purchase of travel medical insurance as per your travel plan.
- Online (http://www.travel-ace.com/ecuador/formularios/vfs/index.php) : Purchase Travel Medical insurance before arriving
                                                                            to visa application Centre.
- Other important information:
- This service will be offered by Travel Ace Assistance at the Visa application centre
- This service is purely optional in nature
- You will have to fill the consent form before purchasing the Insurance at the Visa application Centre
- Product information commercial (http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7IH2SQn9Vdw&list=UUV4-rVBLg9FrRLHjJcBWOpg&index=1&feature=plcp)
- The Travel and Health Insurance is operated by Travel Ace Assistance company and they have the sole responsibility of any claims or issues with the Insurance Policies
- Service will be operational with effect from September 10, 2012

## Processing Time
- Processing times in Ecuador
The Majority of standard visa applications are processed within 3-5 working days in Quito, and 8 working days in provinces, from the date your application is submitted to the
                                                                                Consular Department of the Embassy of Italy in Quito.
Important notes: 
- Visa applications are assessed individually and processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application will depend on several factors and because of
                                                                                    this you should not to make your travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Italy until you have received your Schengen visa.
- Your passport can be retained by the Consular Department of the Italy Embassy in Quito for 30 days for processing of visa application
- You may require to appear for personal interview, should consulate in Quito require
When to prepare your application

The Consular Department of the Embassy of Italy in Ecuador advises you to allow enough time to prepare your visa application. Some applicants can take 1  to 2                                                                                weeks to prepare their visa application and you should allow enough time to read the guidance on the visa category you are applying for, complete your visa application form,
                                                                                prepare your supporting documents and arrange for translations (if necessary). You can submit your visa application up to 3 months in advance of your planned travel date.
                                                                                
Queries about processing times

You can track the status of your application by using the Track your Application facility on this website and by using the SMS text alert facility (fee payable).you can contact
                                                                                Visa Service Center by using the details available on the Contact Us page of this website. 
Role of Visa Application Center in processing

Please note Visa Application Center and none of its staff has any role in the decision making process. Processing of visa application is completed by the staffs at the Embassy
                                                                                of Italy in Quito and Guayaquil.

## Visa Application Form

Italian Version (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/10o5Akws4wJnRXYoBCOttp/c2d2be498e04c14bf2e8dbbd8f02864b/formulario-schengen-italia-nuevo.pdf) 
Spanish Version (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2WIQFeSdg6cDO0Ns1Xz8f9/d500ab8903af3b2b8e8dafabd3e573a2/modello-di-invito-per-affari-nuovo.pdf)
If you want to download the visa application form:
- Click on the link of the form
- When the page appears, save the form on your computer
OR
- Press the right button of your mouse/mouse when the cursor on the appropriate link
- Select< save>
- Save the form on your computer.
